sections.title: A: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each of the numbered words or phrases in bold is incorrect. Rewrite them correctly.

items.sentence: Forecasts might warn **to us** about threats posed by the weather, but imagine if we could take **out** control of the weather and prevent dangerous weather conditions from **in** the first place.

Controlling the weather may be the biggest technological challenge we face. For a **long** of time, scientists have dreamed of creating artificial clouds to bring rain to areas hit by drought, but it's much harder to do than they expected **that**.
The global weather system is very complicated, with each part having an effect **taken** on all the others.
The scientists may feel they are wasting **up** their time, but success could save millions of lives.

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: The forecast said there was a high (LIKELY) ...... of rain this weekend.
We all know that using our cars causes (POLLUTE) ......, but we still do it.
Forecasting the weather (ACCURATE) ...... takes a great deal of training.
Litter is often a problem in (RESIDENT) ...... areas.
According to (ENVIRONMENT) ......, we could be facing a crisis within fifty years.
It was a wonderfully (SUN) ...... day, so we decided to go to the beach.
The giant panda is (DANGER) ...... because its habitat is being destroyed.
We didn't enjoy our walk because it was absolutely (FREEZE) ......!

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
Did you see the documentary about the ozone layer? **saw**
I wonder ...... the documentary about the ozone layer.

I saw a badger for a moment before it disappeared into the woods. **sight**
I ...... a badger before it disappeared into the woods.

I hope the weather gets better for our trip to the mountains this weekend. **up**
I hope ...... for our trip to the mountains this weekend.

Did you hear that the greenhouse in the park had been demolished? **torn**
Did you hear that they ...... the greenhouse in the park?

Everything we do affects the environment in one way or another. **effect**
Everything we do ...... the environment in one way or another.

I don't really know a lot about the work that Greenpeace does. **familiar**
I ...... the work that Greenpeace does.

The builders have spoiled our garden. **mess**
The builders have ...... our garden.

Dr Trent said the problems were caused by rubbish in the streets. **put**
Dr Trent ...... rubbish in the streets.

Most people know that wasting water causes problems. **aware**
Most people ...... the problems caused by wasting water.
No one seems to care about the environment, ......?
Do you know where ...... a book about insects?
The new law prevents people ...... their rubbish in certain areas.
Let's walk to the shops instead of taking the car, ......?
There isn't much point in trying to save electricity, ......?
Don't drop your sweet wrapper on the floor, ......?
'I went to the exhibition at the natural history museum.' 'Oh, yes? What ...... there?'
There's been a ...... in Germany and a village was completely destroyed.
Do you know what CFC ......?
Dinosaurs have been ...... for millions of years.
It's sunny, but there's a very cold ...... so don't forget your coat.
If you ask me, ...... waste is a much bigger problem than ordinary household waste.
As towns grow, they tend to destroy the surrounding ...... areas.
Jill put her wellington boots on and, as soon as it stopped raining, went out to play in the .......
